2605.13527.title: MMSkills: Towards Multimodal Skills for General Visual Agents
2605.13527.paper_summary: Reusable skills have become a core substrate for improving agent capabilities, yet most existing skill packages encode reusable behavior primarily as textual prompts, executable code, or learned routines. For visual agents, however, procedural knowledge is inherently multimodal: reuse depends not only on what operation to perform, but also on recognizing the relevant state, interpreting visual evidence of progress or failure, and deciding what to do next. We formalize this requirement as multimodal procedural knowledge and address three practical challenges: (I) what a multimodal skill package should contain; (II) where such packages can be derived from public interaction experience; and (III) how agents can consult multimodal evidence at inference time without excessive image context or over-anchoring to reference screenshots. We introduce MMSkills, a framework for representing, generating, and using reusable multimodal procedures for runtime visual decision making. Each MMSkill is a compact, state-conditioned package that couples a textual procedure with runtime state cards and multi-view keyframes. To construct these packages, we develop an agentic trajectory-to-skill Generator that transforms public non-evaluation trajectories into reusable multimodal skills through workflow grouping, procedure induction, visual grounding, and meta-skill-guided auditing. To use them, we introduce a branch-loaded multimodal skill agent: selected state cards and keyframes are inspected in a temporary branch, aligned with the live environment, and distilled into structured guidance for the main agent. Experiments across GUI and game-based visual-agent benchmarks show that MMSkills consistently improve both frontier and smaller multimodal agents, suggesting that external multimodal procedural knowledge complements model-internal priors.
2605.13527.paper_summary_zh: 可重複使用的技能已成為提升代理能力的核心基礎，但大多數現有的技能包主要將可重複使用的行為編碼為文本提示、可執行代碼或學習的例程。然而，對於視覺代理來說，程序性知識本質上是多模態的：重用不僅取決於要執行的操作，還取決於識別相關狀態、解釋進展或失敗的視覺證據，以及決定接下來該做什麼。我們將這一要求形式化為多模態程序性知識，並解決三個實際挑戰：（I）多模態技能包應包含什麼；（II）這些包可以從公共互動經驗中衍生出來的地方；以及（III）代理如何在推理時查詢多模態證據，而不需過多的圖像上下文或過度依賴參考截圖。我們介紹了MMSkills，一個用於表示、生成和使用可重複使用的多模態程序以進行運行時視覺決策的框架。每個MMSkill都是一個緊湊的、狀態條件的包，將文本程序與運行時狀態卡和多視角關鍵幀結合在一起。為了構建這些包，我們開發了一個代理軌跡到技能生成器，通過工作流程分組、程序歸納、視覺基礎和元技能指導審核，將公共非評估軌跡轉換為可重複使用的多模態技能。為了使用它們，我們介紹了一個分支加載的多模態技能代理：選定的狀態卡和關鍵幀在臨時分支中檢查，與實時環境對齊，並提煉成主要代理的結構化指導。在GUI和基於遊戲的視覺代理基準測試中的實驗顯示，MMSkills始終改善了前沿和較小的多模態代理，這表明外部多模態程序性知識補充了模型內部的先驗知識。
2605.13527.author: Kangning Zhang et.al.
2605.13527.authors: Kangning Zhang, Shuai Shao, Qingyao Li, Jianghao Lin, Lingyue Fu, Shijian Wang, Wenxiang Jiao, Yuan Lu, Weiwen Liu, Weinan Zhang, Yong Yu
